steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The graph shows $y = f(x) + 2$. Reading the graph, as $x \to -\infty$ the curve approaches a horizontal asymptote at $y = 5$. Since $f(x) + 2$ has asymptote 5, $f(x)$ itself has asymptote $5 - 2 = 3$. Every answer choice has the form $f(x) = -3^{x} + c$; as $x \to -\infty$, $-3^{x} \to 0$, so the asymptote equals $c$. We need $c = 3$.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — $f(x)=-3^{x}+1$: asymptote at $y = 1$, not 3. ✗
  • Choice B — $f(x)=-3^{x}+3$: asymptote at $y = 3$. Check: $f(1) = -3 + 3 = 0$, so $f(1)+2 = 2$ — matches the graph. ✓
  • Choice C — $f(x)=-3^{x}+4$: asymptote at $y = 4$, not 3. ✗
  • Choice D — $f(x)=-3^{x}+5$: asymptote at $y = 5$, not 3. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
